title: Stelis janetiae
taxonomy: Stelis janetiae is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. The genus Stelis belongs to the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a subtribe of the orchid subfamily Epidendroideae. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is part of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and taxonomic hierarchy of the plant. The exact classification of Stelis janetiae indicates its place within the complex system of plant classification.

intro: Stelis janetiae, also known as Janet's Stelis, is a type of orchid that grows in the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Stelis janetiae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stelis janetiae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.
